suppress.notes: CVE-2017-12796: Unauthenticated XML deserialization RCE in the Reporting Compatibility Add On (reportingcompatibility module) before 2.0.4, as shipped in OpenMRS Reference Application before 2.6.1. This finding is a false positive if the scanned artifact is not the reportingcompatibility module itself. The CVE scanner incorrectly matches on the shared openmrs namespace rather than the specific affected artifact.

CVE-2021-43094: SQL injection via arbitrary GET parameters in patient.page, affecting OpenMRS Reference Application Standalone Edition <=2.11 and Platform Standalone Edition <=2.4.0. This is a false positive for individual module JARs — the vulnerability is in the standalone distribution packaging, not in module code. The scanner matches on the shared openmrs namespace.

CVE-2020-5728: XSS via unsanitized Referrer header copied into the redirectUrl field on multiple web pages (e.g. login.htm) in openmrs-core 2.9 and prior. This is a false positive for module JARs — the vulnerable code lives in the core web layer, not in any module. The scanner matches on the shared openmrs namespace.

CVE-2020-5729: XSS via the UI Framework Error Page reflecting arbitrary user input in openmrs-core 2.9 and prior. This is a false positive for module JARs — the vulnerable rendering logic belongs to openmrs-core's UI framework, not to any module. The scanner matches on the shared openmrs namespace.

CVE-2020-5730: XSS via the sessionLocation parameter on the login page in openmrs-core 2.9 and prior. This is a false positive for module JARs — the vulnerable parameter is handled by the core login controller, not by any module. The scanner matches on the shared openmrs namespace.

CVE-2020-5731: XSS via the app parameter on the ActiveVisits page in openmrs-core 2.9 and prior. This is a false positive for module JARs — the vulnerable page and parameter handling are part of openmrs-core, not any module. The scanner matches on the shared openmrs namespace.

CVE-2020-5732: Missing authentication on the import endpoint of the Data Exchange Module in openmrs-core 2.9 and prior. This is a false positive if the scanned artifact is not the dataexchange module itself. The access control bypass is specific to that module's controller; the scanner incorrectly matches other JARs via the shared openmrs namespace.

CVE-2020-5733: Missing authentication on the export endpoint of the Data Exchange Module in openmrs-core 2.9 and prior, allowing unauthenticated users to export potentially sensitive data. This is a false positive if the scanned artifact is not the dataexchange module itself. Like CVE-2020-5732, the vulnerability is specific to that module's controller; the scanner incorrectly matches other JARs via the shared openmrs namespace.

CVE-2026-40076: Zip Slip path traversal in the module upload endpoint (POST /openmrs/ws/rest/v1/module) of openmrs-core 2.7.8 and prior and 2.8.0 through 2.8.5, allowing an authenticated attacker with module upload access to write files outside the module directory and achieve RCE. Fixed in openmrs-core 2.8.6. This is a false positive for module JARs — the vulnerable extraction logic lives in openmrs-core, not in any module. The scanner matches on the shared openmrs namespace.

CVE-2026-40075: Path traversal in the /openmrs/moduleResources/{moduleid} endpoint of openmrs-core 2.7.8 and prior and 2.8.0 through 2.8.5. ModuleResourcesServlet builds a filesystem path from user-controlled input without boundary validation, and because the endpoint is unauthenticated it allows reading arbitrary files. Fixed in openmrs-core 2.8.6. This is a false positive for module JARs — the vulnerable servlet belongs to openmrs-core, not to any module. The scanner matches on the shared openmrs namespace.

co.elastic.clients:elasticsearch-java is the Java REST client library, not the Elasticsearch server. The scanner's CPE matcher conflates the client jar with cpe:/a:elastic:elasticsearch and cpe:/a:elasticsearch:elasticsearch (the server product), so every server-only CVE published against that Elasticsearch version is falsely attributed to the client jar (e.g. CVE-2024-23445, CVE-2024-52979/52980/52981, CVE-2025-37731, CVE-2025-68384 — all server-side: cross-cluster API keys, query StackOverflow/DoS in the server, etc.). Suppress the two server CPEs for the client jar rather than enumerating CVEs, so the suppression survives client version bumps and future server-only CVEs. A genuine client-library CVE would carry the co.elastic.clients coordinate (or a client-specific CPE), not these server CPEs, so it still surfaces.
